title: DIY Vase Holder
summary: This wooden vase holder is a simple woodworking project (great for beginners!) and a fun decorative accent to any home.
description: This wooden vase holder is a simple woodworking project (great for beginners!) and a fun decorative accent to any home.

steps.title: Cut Your Wood
steps.body: Cut your 1x4 board to the following dimensions (1) at 22” long (2) at 5” long

steps.title: Drill Pocket Holes
steps.body: Use the Kreg Jig 520PRO to drill 2 pocket holes at one end of each of your 5” long boards.

steps.title: Drill Holes for Vases
steps.body: Use a 2” Hole Saw Bit to drill holes along your 22” long board. First, measure and mark your center point. Drill your hole. From there, measure and mark so that the center of each hole is 4” apart (the outer edge of each hole should be 2” apart).

steps.title: Sand
steps.body: Sand your boards and use a small sanding block or sheet of sandpaper to smooth out the edges and insides of each hole.

steps.title: Attach End Pieces
steps.body: Use 1 ¼” pocket hole screws to attach the 5” long end pieces to your 22” long board.

steps.title: Paint or Stain
steps.body: Finish your vase holder however you choose. For this one, I used a Provincial stain.

steps.title: Insert Vases and Add Flowers
steps.body: Add whatever flowers or greenery you choose and enjoy your new decor piece!
